title: 6-Board Leopold Bench
summary: A classic outdoor bench built from three boards and one angled cut — no joinery beyond screws.
description:
The Leopold bench is about as simple as outdoor seating gets: six pieces cut from three boards, held together with screws at a single 30° angle. No mortises, no dowels, nothing to glue.

The **angle is the whole design.** Cutting the leg tops at 30 degrees off square is what gives the seat its slight recline and the bench its name and silhouette. Get that one angle consistent across all four leg pieces and the rest of the build is just square crosscuts and screws.

Built from construction lumber, it costs next to nothing and takes an afternoon — which is why it shows up on back porches, at campsites, and in scout-troop projects everywhere.

tools.note: Every leg piece shares the same 30° angle — set it once and don't touch it until all four are cut.

cutList.note: Both ends cut 30° off square, ends parallel.
Both ends cut 30° off square, ends parallel.

steps.title: Cut all six pieces
steps.body:
Cut the two long legs to 35" and two short legs to 18", angling both ends of every leg piece 30° off square, ends parallel. Cut the backrest (48") and seat (45") square — no angles on those two.

Cut every angled piece in the same saw session without adjusting the angle setting. That consistency is what makes both leg assemblies match.

steps.title: Build the two leg assemblies
steps.body: Stand a short leg against a long leg, align the top edges, and screw them together. Repeat for the second pair — you now have two matching leg assemblies with the angled cut giving each one a lean.

steps.title: Attach the backrest
steps.body: Screw the 2x6 backrest across the tops of the two leg assemblies, keeping the top edges flush. This ties the two leg assemblies together and sets the bench's overall width.

steps.title: Attach the seat
steps.body: Set the frame on a flat surface and screw the 2x10 seat across the tops of the legs, centered side to side, driving screws from both the top face and the outside face of the long legs for a solid connection.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand all edges and corners smooth, then finish with an exterior stain or paint rated for outdoor use.
